MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jane Yee of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715), appearing for defendant / moving party Ric-Wil, Incorporated
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Elvin Gazzano

RULING:
DEFENDANT RIC-WIL, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he could properly identify Ric-Wil pipe or any product or material attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections based upon hearsay to plaintiff's declaration where he identified Ric-Wil piping based upon a review of statements in written specifications are sustained. In view of the court's sustaining defendant's objections, the objections to the declarations of Mr. Ay and Dr. Cohen are sustained as speculative and lacking foundation. The Court did not consider Mr. Spielman's Declaration. Order after hearing, and orders on both plaintiff's and defendant's evidentiary objections were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
